title: OCIRuntime field in SpecGenerator is ignored
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,

When trying to create containers with the `crun-krun` runtime with the API I stumbled accros a bug where the OCIRuntime field is ignored in the final container spec. Looking more carefully with the help of an LLM, it looks like the code [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/specgen/generate/container_create.go#L168C1-L175C3) does not use `s.OCIRuntime` at all, which could explain the behaviour.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a container with OCIRuntime set to a valid runtime different from the default one
2. Observe that the container uses the default runtime from containers.conf instead


### Describe the results you received

The created container uses the default runtime from containers.conf and not the runtime as asked, but creating the same container with `podman` cli and the `--runtime` flag works as expected

### Describe the results you expected

Container using the specified runtime.

comments.body:
> with the API I stumbled across a bug where the OCIRuntime field is ignored in the final container spec.

You mean the HTTP REST API? A quick check in the code seems to suggest that the OCIRuntime field is indeed ignored so it was never implemented. That should be easy enough to fix.

CreateExitCommandArgs() also seem to be hard coded on the config runtime and not actually using the one from the container config, I suppose it does not matter there as the cleanup code uses the runtime from the container config regardless of the --runtime option.

Yes I mean the HTTP REST API, sorry for the confusion.

Thanks !
